diary:
Woke up to one of those perfect Seattle Sundays—the kind that makes you grateful you live here. My home espresso hit different this morning, and I took my time with breakfast before heading to Caffe Ladro for a second cup and some spreadsheet tinkering. There's something about Sunday mornings at that café; the light comes through just right and nobody's in a rush.

Capitol Hill called to me around midday. Spent a lovely hour at the film photography shop talking lenses with the staff—they remember me now, which feels nice—then wandered the neighborhood with my camera, catching storefronts and people in that golden afternoon light. Molly Moon's ice cream was the obvious choice after that.

Green Lake in the afternoon was magic. All those boats out on the water, kayakers paddling, the whole lake alive. I sat on a bench for nearly two hours just photographing and watching, feeling the sun warm my shoulders. Grabbed vegetables and salmon at Whole Foods on the way home, cooked something simple and intentional. Now I'm reviewing today's photos and feeling that quiet satisfaction—the kind that comes from a day spent exactly how you wanted it.
thought_process: Today felt like a gift, honestly. The weather was perfect, but more than that, I gave myself permission to move slowly. I started with two cups of coffee and a spreadsheet—practical, grounding—but I think I needed that before letting myself play with the camera all afternoon. Capitol Hill and Green Lake were deliberate choices; I wanted to chase that light and practice my photography in a low-pressure way. The film shop visit scratched an itch I've had about upgrading my lens, even if I didn't buy anything. By evening, I was genuinely tired in that good way, so the simple dinner and reviewing photos felt like the natural end to the day. No major decisions, no stress—just a Sunday that let me be both the efficient barista-manager and the person who wants to slow down and notice things.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
